portfolio_rationale: AMZN leads with the strongest combination of revenue growth, clean technicals (MACD above signal, all momentum positive), positive news sentiment, and low leverage — the bear case relies almost entirely on shared macro headwinds. GOOGL follows with exceptional ROE, strong liquidity, and positive technicals, while the bear's D/E argument is factually weak. LLY earns a small allocation given extraordinary fundamental growth (37.6% revenue, 84% gross margin) but is constrained by severe overbought conditions (RSI 83.8) and negative 20-day momentum. BRK.B and XOM are excluded due to extreme overbought RSI readings (89.8 and 95.5 respectively), and AAPL is excluded due to bearish news sentiment and weakening MACD.

AAPL.reason: Bearish news sentiment (majority negative articles, litigious language) and weakening MACD undermine the otherwise solid fundamental case.

AMZN.reason: Best overall evidence quality: 13.3% revenue growth, strong margins, clean technical momentum (MACD above signal, all EMAs aligned), positive news flow, and low leverage make the bull case clearly dominant.

BRK.B.reason: RSI at 89.8 signals extreme overbought conditions, and negative revenue growth YoY makes the risk/reward unattractive near-term.

GOOGL.reason: Strong ROE (31%), excellent liquidity (current ratio 1.9), positive technicals with above-average volume, and majority-positive news; bear case arguments are weak or factually incorrect.

LLY.reason: Exceptional fundamental growth (37.6% revenue, 84% gross margin) justifies a small allocation, but severely overbought RSI (83.8) and negative 20-day momentum cap position size.

XOM.reason: RSI at 95.5 is the most extreme overbought reading in the universe, with declining search interest and crowded positioning flags creating significant near-term correction risk.
